- The warmth in your leg, alongside swelling reported previously, requires immediate medical evaluation to rule out serious underlying conditions.

WHY THIS NEEDS ESCALATION:
- Swelling in the legs can be a symptom of cardiac disorders (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
- Warmth and swelling together may indicate inflammation or circulatory issues requiring prompt diagnosis (24052018_CCRAS_Cardiac_disorders).
- The duration of two weeks for these symptoms warrants immediate investigation to prevent potential complications (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).
- Use of ice to alleviate symptoms is a temporary measure and does not address the underlying cause (ayurvedic_treatment_file1).

WHAT NOT TO DO NOW:
- Do not self-treat with home remedies without a professional diagnosis.
- Do not ignore the warmth or swelling, hoping it will resolve on its own.
- Do not engage in strenuous physical activity.

WHAT TO DO NOW:
- Seek immediate medical attention from a qualified healthcare provider.
- Describe all symptoms, including the warmth, swelling, and duration, to the healthcare professional.
- Follow the healthcare provider’s recommendations for diagnosis and treatment.
